palette_hint: Deep navy background #0a0f2e with off-white text #e8edf5, accented by bioluminescent cyan #00d4ff and coral-warning orange #ff6b35
type_style: Headings in system Georgia serif at heavy weight for gravitas, body in system Arial at 400 weight, tight letter-spacing for a scientific-dashboard feel
description: Deep Current is a five-page interactive infographic exploring the ocean's hidden layers — from surface temperatures to hadal trenches — using SVG-driven data visualizations and non-rectangular geometry to communicate marine science facts.
constraints: Every page uses a full-bleed #0a0f2e background with a persistent SVG wave-divider (sinusoidal path) separating the hero section from content below, colored #0d1a45
All section containers are clipped with clip-path: polygon() cuts at 4–8 degree angles on at least one edge, maximum content width 1100px centered with auto margins
Data callout boxes use SVG hexagon shapes (drawn via polygon points) filled with rgba(0,212,255,0.12) and stroked with #00d4ff at 1.5px
Decorative depth-ruler SVG lines run vertically on the left margin of each page, marked at 200m / 1000m / 4000m / 11000m with horizontal tick marks and #e8edf5 labels at 11px
Accent icons (fish, pressure gauge, thermometer, coral) are drawn entirely from inline SVG paths — no external images or emoji — and must be at least 48x48px viewBox

page_specs.overview: The landing page features a large inline SVG cross-section illustration of the ocean showing five named depth zones (Sunlight 0–200m, Twilight 200–1000m, Midnight 1000–4000m, Abyssal 4000–6000m, Hadal 6000–11000m) as stacked trapezoid shapes with gradient fills from #00d4ff at the surface fading to #03010a at the hadal zone. A bold stat strip displays three key numbers — '71% of Earth's surface', '3.68 km average depth', '95% unexplored' — each inside a skewed parallelogram badge using CSS transform: skewX(-8deg). Navigation to all five pages appears as an SVG-outlined horizontal strip with angled tab shapes.
page_specs.temperature: This page visualizes ocean temperature data using a horizontal SVG bar-chart where bars are drawn as parallelogram paths (not rectangles) showing temperatures at each depth zone: 28°C surface, 8°C at 200m, 4°C at 1000m, 2°C at 4000m, 1.5°C at hadal. A secondary SVG illustration shows a thermometer icon with a mercury-fill path animated purely via CSS stroke-dashoffset transitions. Three callout hexagons explain thermocline formation, thermohaline circulation, and the role of polar meltwater, each with a small inline SVG arrow-path pointing to the relevant zone on the chart.
page_specs.biodiversity: A central inline SVG radial diagram shows 12 species arranged in concentric rings by depth zone — anglerfish, viperfish, giant squid, and bioluminescent jellyfish in the midnight zone — with each species represented by a unique 48x48 path-drawn icon connected to its zone ring by curved SVG path lines (cubic bezier). A facts panel clipped at a 6-degree diagonal bottom edge lists five record-holders: largest animal (blue whale, 33m), deepest fish (snailfish, 8336m), most bioluminescent density (twilight zone, 90% of organisms). Species icons use mask-image with radial SVG gradients to create a glow effect in cyan #00d4ff.
page_specs.pressure-zones: The page opens with a vertically-oriented SVG pressure gauge illustration showing pressure increasing from 1 atm at the surface to 1086 atm at the Mariana Trench floor, with a needle path rotating via CSS transform: rotate() at five labeled stops. Below it, five angled content panels (clip-path: polygon cuts at 5 degrees on top and bottom) each describe one depth zone's pressure in human-scale analogies — 'equivalent to 50 jumbo jets resting on your shoulders at 11,000m'. A small inline SVG cross-section of a submarine hull with labeled crush-depth markers at 600m (tourist sub), 730m (military), and 11,000m (Deepsea Challenger) provides visual reference.
page_specs.human-impact: This page uses an SVG-illustrated timeline running diagonally across the page at 8 degrees (CSS transform: rotate(-8deg) on the timeline spine path) marking key events: 1960 first Mariana Trench dive, 1972 Ocean Dumping Act, 1998 coral bleaching event, 2019 plastic found at 11,000m depth. Flanking the timeline, three warning-stat boxes styled with orange #ff6b35 stroke hexagons display '8 million metric tons of plastic per year', '50% of coral reefs lost since 1950', and '90% of large fish populations depleted'. A footer SVG shows a shoreline silhouette clipped with clip-path: ellipse() revealing an underwater scene beneath, with a call-to-action text block for ocean conservation organizations.
